data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-828a1b8f">
    <title>specs: worktree resource-isolation expectations (build-cache reuse, I/O budgets) for ddx try/work</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM: `ddx try` creates a fresh git worktree under
`~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<attempt>/` per attempt
(FEAT-010 layer 2). For projects with native build toolchains (Rust + cargo,
JVM + gradle, Go + module cache, etc.), the fresh worktree starts with an
empty toolchain build cache, so the agent's first build inside is a cold
build from scratch. FEAT-010 §Layer 2 and TD-010 §Storage Model are silent
on this — they specify worktree creation and evidence capture but make no
statement about toolchain build-cache sharing or per-worktree I/O budgets.

OBSERVED COST (2026-05-28, niflheim project):
- A single in-flight `ddx try` attempt accumulated `target/` = 14 GB in the
  execute-bead worktree.
- Two concurrent workers + the project's own niflheim recovery run
  collectively wrote ~23 GB to the same SSD over ~30 minutes.
- The project's WAL recovery (read-bound, ~2 MB written) was visibly
  starved by worker cargo build I/O.
- Each fresh attempt repeats this cost — the worktree is destroyed at
  attempt end (or preserved with `--no-merge`), so the cache is lost.

ROOT CAUSE (spec-level): FEAT-010 treats worktree resource cost as a
non-concern. The feature explicitly lists "workspace creation, bead
attempt, merge or preserve, evidence bundle" but lists no resource-budget
or cache-reuse hook surface. There is no ADR governing per-worktree I/O,
no documented hook for projects to opt into shared toolchain caches, and
no operator-visible counter that would surface this contention in
`ddx work status` or `ddx doctor`.

PROPOSED EVOLUTION (the executing agent should choose how to slot these
into the artifact stack; below is the recommended shape):

1. **Amend FEAT-010 §Layer 2** (`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md`):
   - Add a "Worktree resource expectations" subsection acknowledging that
     execute-bead worktrees do not, by default, share native toolchain
     build caches with the parent checkout or with sibling attempts.
   - State the operator's responsibility (set per-project env like
     `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, `GRADLE_USER_HOME`, `GOPATH/pkg/mod`, etc., or
     project-level config) versus DDx's responsibility (pass through
     environment unchanged, do not invent toolchain-specific knobs).
   - Document that `ddx work` makes no concurrency guarantee for native
     build steps inside attempts; concurrent workers may contend.

2. **Amend TD-010** (`docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md`):
   - Add a §Worktree environment passthrough subsection naming
     `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, `GRADLE_USER_HOME`, `MAVEN_OPTS`, `GOPATH`,
     `GOMODCACHE`, `npm_config_cache`, `PIP_CACHE_DIR`, etc. as
     environment variables that flow unmodified from the `ddx try`
     caller into the agent process inside the worktree.
   - Specify the invariant: DDx never sets, overrides, or unsets any
     toolchain cache env var. Operator/project owns the policy.

3. **Optionally add an ADR** (e.g., `ADR-027-worktree-resource-isolation.md`)
   recording the decision to keep build-cache policy outside DDx, with the
   rationale (cross-language, single-responsibility, prevents lock-in to a
   specific toolchain). If the executing agent decides one ADR is overkill,
   adding the same paragraph as a §Rationale block at the end of TD-010 is
   acceptable.

4. **Update CLAUDE.md "Execution Evidence Convention" section** if needed
   to cross-reference the new TD-010 environment-passthrough subsection
   so agents working in execute-bead worktrees know they may set
   `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (or equivalent) per project policy.

NON-SCOPE:
- Implementing build-cache mounting, sccache integration, or any
  toolchain-specific knob inside ddx itself. This bead is documentation
  only — it locks down the boundary so future implementation work has
  a clear policy to refer to.
- Changing `ddx try` worktree creation semantics or evidence capture.
- Changing the worktree path under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.
- Adding `ddx doctor` or `ddx work status` instrumentation for disk
  contention (potential follow-up bead, not this one).
- Project-side fixes (niflheim has its own bead niflheim-e5cd7c69 for
  the cargo-target-dir workflow documentation).

IN-SCOPE FILES:
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md
- Optionally: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-worktree-resource-isolation.md
  (new) — only if the agent chooses the ADR path over TD-010 §Rationale.
- Optionally: CLAUDE.md (cross-reference paragraph only, no behavior change).

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:
- Any cli/ code — this is a docs-only evolution.
- Other FEAT/TD/ADR documents — touch only the listed artifacts unless
  the agent discovers a direct conflicting claim in another doc, in
  which case file a follow-up bead rather than expanding scope here.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `grep -nE 'Worktree resource expectations|resource expectations|build cache|CARGO_TARGET_DIR' docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md` returns matches for the new subsection.
2. `grep -nE 'environment passthrough|CARGO_TARGET_DIR|GRADLE_USER_HOME|toolchain cache' docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md` returns matches for the new subsection.
3. `git diff --stat HEAD~1 HEAD -- docs/` shows changes only under `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md`, `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md`, optionally `docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-worktree-resource-isolation.md`, and optionally `CLAUDE.md`.
4. `git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD` does not show any file under `cli/`.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:execution, area:specs, kind:doc, phase:iterate, spec:FEAT-010, spec:TD-010</labels>
    <metadata spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="ff96effedc3039e77a7485862a779150913762c2" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260530T060534-0a0029f6"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed the bead.

Commit: `b10bc041c docs: specify worktree cache passthrough [ddx-828a1b8f]`

Changed only:
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md`
- `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md`

Verification passed:
- FEAT-010 grep AC matched `build cache` / `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`
- TD-010 grep AC matched `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, `GRADLE_USER_HOME`, `toolchain cache`
- `git diff --stat HEAD~1 HEAD -- docs/` shows only the two in-scope docs
- `git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD` shows no `cli/` files
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passed, including during commit hook execution
- Worktree is clean
